description: SaaS-Grade Frontend Redesign + Admin Backend — Warm Obsidian design language, motion system, page redesigns, role-based admin with user management, settings, analytics, and audit logging

userStories.title: Install Plus Jakarta Sans and update font tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the new display font installed and referenced in CSS tokens so that all headings use Plus Jakarta Sans.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @fontsource/plus-jakarta-sans (weights 400, 500, 600, 700) via npm
Update src/web/src/index.css: replace @fontsource/nunito import with @fontsource/plus-jakarta-sans
Update --font-display from 'Nunito' to 'Plus Jakarta Sans'
Verify --font-body remains 'Inter'
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Spec: tasks/design-tokens.md → Typography section

userStories.title: Implement new color palette tokens (light + dark)
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the Warm Obsidian color palette applied to all CSS custom properties for both light and dark modes.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace all color values in :root (light mode) block in index.css with Warm Obsidian light palette from tasks/design-tokens.md
Replace all color values in .dark block with Warm Obsidian dark palette from tasks/design-tokens.md
Add new tokens: --warning, --warning-foreground, --info, --info-foreground
Update sidebar tokens (--sidebar-*) for both modes
Add chart color tokens (--chart-1 through --chart-8) for both modes
Update focus ring color to amber (--ring)
Update text selection color (::selection styles)
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Spec: tasks/design-tokens.md → Color Palette section. Use stone scale for neutrals, amber for accent.

userStories.title: Implement spacing, border-radius, and elevation tokens
userStories.description: As a developer, I need updated spacing, border-radius, and shadow tokens in CSS and Tailwind theme.
acceptanceCriteria: Add spacing tokens to @theme inline: --space-0.5 (2px), --space-1.5 (6px), --space-16 (64px), --space-20 (80px), --space-24 (96px)
Update border-radius: --radius-md → 8px, --radius-lg → 12px; add --radius-2xl (24px)
Add shadow tokens to :root and .dark: --shadow-sm, --shadow-md, --shadow-lg, --shadow-xl with warm-tinted light and high-opacity dark values
Reference shadow tokens in @theme inline for Tailwind integration
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Spec: tasks/design-tokens.md → Spacing, Border Radius, Elevation sections

userStories.title: Implement motion tokens and update motion.tsx
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the motion system tokens (durations, springs, easings) defined and the motion.tsx DS component updated with reduced-motion support.
acceptanceCriteria: Add CSS custom properties to :root in index.css: --duration-instant (50ms), --duration-fast (150ms), --duration-normal (250ms), --duration-slow (400ms), --duration-deliberate (600ms), --ease-standard, --ease-enter, --ease-exit, --ease-emphasized
Update src/web/src/components/ds/motion.tsx: export springs object (default, gentle, bouncy, stiff), durations object, easings object
Update existing variants (PageTransition, FadeIn, StaggerChildren, ScaleIn) to use new tokens
Add getVariants() factory that returns noop variants when prefers-reduced-motion is set
Replace any Tailwind animate-pulse usage with custom @keyframes skeleton-pulse that respects reduced motion
Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block in index.css that disables CSS animations but preserves spinners
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
userStories.notes: Spec: tasks/design-motion.md → Duration Tokens, Easing Curves, Spring Configs sections

Rotate -90deg to start at 12 o'clock
Color tiers: <50% muted, 50-99% accent, 100% success with glow (filter: drop-shadow)
100% completion: one-time scale pulse via Framer Motion spring
CSS transition for smooth fill animation
Reduced motion: instant fill (no animation)

userStories.title: Add role-based identity system
userStories.description: As a platform owner, I need role-based access control so that admin features are restricted to authorized users.
acceptanceCriteria: Change ApplicationDbContext from IdentityUserContext<User> to IdentityDbContext<User, IdentityRole, string>
Create EF Core migration to add role tables (AspNetRoles, AspNetUserRoles, AspNetRoleClaims)
Update ServiceCollectionExtensions: change AddIdentityCore<User>() to AddIdentityCore<User>().AddRoles<IdentityRole>().AddEntityFrameworkStores<ApplicationDbContext>()
Add authorization policy: builder.Services.AddAuthorizationBuilder().AddPolicy('AdminOnly', p => p.RequireRole('Admin'))
Update AuthService JWT generation: add ClaimTypes.Role claim from user's roles
Seed Admin and User roles on startup via backend data seeding
Add GET /api/auth/me enhancement: include role in the response DTO
Integration tests: verify admin role assignment, JWT includes role claim, admin policy rejects non-admin users
dotnet build and dotnet test pass
userStories.notes: Current state: No role field on User entity, no role tables in DB. JWT tokens contain only NameIdentifier, Email, Jti. IdentityUserContext must become IdentityDbContext to restore role support.

userStories.title: Create audit log entity and infrastructure
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the audit log entity, repository, and service created as infrastructure for logging system activity.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Entities/Admin/AuditLog.cs: Id (int), Timestamp (DateTime), UserId (string, FK), Action (string), TargetType (string), TargetId (string, nullable), Details (string, JSON), IpAddress (string, nullable)
Add DbSet<AuditLog> to ApplicationDbContext; create EF Core migration
Create IAuditLogRepository + AuditLogRepository with methods: LogAsync, GetPagedAsync(filter, page, pageSize), GetAllFilteredAsync(filter) for CSV export
Create IAuditLogService + AuditLogService with LogActionAsync(userId, action, targetType, targetId, details, ipAddress)
Register in DI (ServiceCollectionExtensions)
Integration tests for audit log creation
dotnet build and dotnet test pass
userStories.notes: This story creates the infrastructure only. Wiring into existing services happens in US-030.

userStories.title: Wire audit logging into existing services
userStories.description: As an admin, I need all CRUD operations and auth events automatically logged to the audit trail.
acceptanceCriteria: Wire audit logging into CollectionsService: create, update, delete actions
Wire audit logging into CatalogItemsService: create, update, delete actions
Wire audit logging into OwnedCopiesService: create, delete actions
Wire audit logging into SetsService: create, update, delete actions
Wire audit logging into WishlistService: create, update, delete actions
Wire audit logging into AuthService: Login (success/failure), Register
Integration tests verifying audit entries are created for CRUD operations
dotnet build and dotnet test pass
userStories.notes: Depends on US-029. Inject IAuditLogService into each existing service.

userStories.title: Create system settings infrastructure
userStories.description: As an admin, I need configurable system settings stored in the database with seeded defaults.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Entities/Admin/SystemSetting.cs: Id (int), Key (string, unique), Value (string), Category (string), UpdatedAt (DateTime), UpdatedById (string, FK)
Add DbSet<SystemSetting> to ApplicationDbContext; create EF Core migration
Create ISystemSettingsRepository + SystemSettingsRepository: GetByCategoryAsync, GetByKeyAsync, UpsertAsync, GetAllAsync
Create ISystemSettingsService + SystemSettingsService: GetSettingsByCategory, UpdateSettings

Seed default settings on startup: General (appName, language, currency), Security (minPasswordLength, requireUppercase, requireNumber, requireSpecial, sessionTimeoutMinutes, registrationEnabled), Features (wishlistEnabled, importExportEnabled, imageUploadsEnabled, publicProfilesEnabled, valueTrackingEnabled), Storage (maxUploadSizeMb, allowedFileTypes)
Register in DI
Integration tests for settings CRUD
dotnet build and dotnet test pass
userStories.notes: Spec: tasks/design-admin.md → System Settings

userStories.title: Implement admin user management API
userStories.description: As an admin, I need API endpoints to manage platform users — list, view, change roles, disable accounts.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Controllers/Admin/AdminUsersController.cs (minimal API style, MapAdminUsersEndpoints())
All endpoints require .RequireAuthorization('AdminOnly')
GET /api/admin/users — paginated, searchable (name/email), filterable (role, status), sortable
GET /api/admin/users/{id} — user detail with collection stats (count, total items, total value)
PUT /api/admin/users/{id}/role — change role (Admin/User); prevent removing last admin
PUT /api/admin/users/{id}/status — enable/disable user (add IsDisabled bool to User entity + migration)
DELETE /api/admin/users/{id} — soft-delete (IsDeleted + DeletedAt on User entity); prevent self-deletion
Create DTOs, IAdminUsersRepository + AdminUsersRepository, IAdminUsersService + AdminUsersService
All actions logged via AuditLogService
Integration tests for all endpoints (happy path + authorization + edge cases)
dotnet build and dotnet test pass
userStories.notes: Spec: tasks/design-admin.md → User Management. Soft-deleted users excluded from queries by default.

userStories.title: Implement admin system settings API
userStories.description: As an admin, I need API endpoints to read and update system settings by category.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Controllers/Admin/AdminSettingsController.cs
All endpoints require .RequireAuthorization('AdminOnly')
GET /api/admin/settings — returns all settings grouped by category
GET /api/admin/settings/{category} — returns settings for one category
PUT /api/admin/settings/{category} — update settings for a category (validates known keys, rejects unknown)
Create DTOs: SettingsCategoryResponse, UpdateSettingsRequest
Settings changes logged via AuditLogService with before/after values in details
Integration tests for CRUD + authorization
dotnet build and dotnet test pass
userStories.notes: Spec: tasks/design-admin.md → System Settings

userStories.title: Implement admin analytics API
userStories.description: As an admin, I need analytics endpoints to view platform usage statistics and growth trends.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Controllers/Admin/AdminAnalyticsController.cs
All endpoints require .RequireAuthorization('AdminOnly')
GET /api/admin/analytics/stats?range=30d — Total Users, Active Users (within range), Total Collections, Total Items
GET /api/admin/analytics/growth?range=30d — daily user signups and collections created grouped by day
GET /api/admin/analytics/usage?range=30d — most active users (top 5), largest collections (top 5), popular collection types
Range parameter: 7d, 30d, 90d, all (default 30d)
Create DTOs, IAdminAnalyticsRepository + AdminAnalyticsRepository, IAdminAnalyticsService + AdminAnalyticsService
Integration tests for all endpoints with range filtering
dotnet build and dotnet test pass
userStories.notes: Spec: tasks/design-admin.md → Analytics Dashboard

userStories.title: Implement admin audit log API
userStories.description: As an admin, I need to query, filter, and export the audit log via API endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: Create Controllers/Admin/AdminAuditLogController.cs
All endpoints require .RequireAuthorization('AdminOnly')
GET /api/admin/audit-log — paginated, filterable: search, action type, userId, fromDate, toDate; sortable by timestamp (desc default)
GET /api/admin/audit-log/{id} — single entry detail with full metadata
GET /api/admin/audit-log/export?format=csv — returns CSV of all filtered entries, filename geekvault-audit-log-{yyyy-MM-dd}.csv
Create DTOs: AuditLogEntryResponse, AuditLogListResponse, AuditLogFilterRequest
Integration tests for pagination, filtering, and CSV export
dotnet build and dotnet test pass

userStories.title: Implement admin layout and sidebar context switch
userStories.description: As an admin, I want the sidebar to context-switch to admin navigation when on /admin routes.
acceptanceCriteria: Update sidebar: define adminNavGroups alongside navGroups, switch based on location.pathname.startsWith('/admin')
Admin nav items: Back to GeekVault (header link), Management group (Users, Settings), Insights group (Analytics, Audit Log)
AnimatePresence fast fade (100ms) on nav group container
Visual differentiation: sidebar 'ADMIN' badge (Shield icon), toolbar admin pill badge (amber-tinted)
Mobile header text: 'Admin' instead of 'GeekVault' when on admin routes
Route guard: redirect non-admin users silently to / (no toast)
/admin redirects to /admin/users
Create src/web/src/features/admin/ directory; add routes to App.tsx
Add new i18n keys to both en.json and pt.json
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)

userStories.title: Global accessibility and dark mode audit
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to audit and fix all accessibility and dark mode issues across the entire app.
acceptanceCriteria: Replace all :focus with :focus-visible in CSS and component classes
Add aria-label to all icon-only buttons across all pages
Add tabIndex={0}, role, onKeyDown to all clickable divs
Verify WCAG AA contrast on all text/accent combos in both modes
Verify no hardcoded colors remain (grep for hex, rgb(), hsl() without var())
Verify dark mode on every page — no invisible elements, proper shadows, chart legibility
Test prefers-reduced-motion: reduce — no transforms, spinners work
Typecheck passes (npx tsc -b)
All tests pass (npm test)
userStories.notes: Spec: tasks/design-audit.md → Accessibility Gaps, Dark Mode Assessment

userStories.title: Final cross-page consistency review
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify consistency across all pages and run the full test/build suite.
acceptanceCriteria: Section spacing consistent: --space-8 desktop, --space-6 mobile across all pages
Empty states use EmptyState component consistently (no pages returning null)
Card radius: --radius-xl for collection gallery, --radius-lg for others
Hover patterns: lift on navigable cards, shadow-only on data cards
Skeleton loading uses custom pulse keyframe everywhere
All chart fills use hsl(var(--chart-N))
All UI strings in both en.json and pt.json
Run npm test — all pass
Run npm run test:coverage — 90% line coverage
Run npx tsc -b — zero errors
Run dotnet test — all pass
Run dotnet build — success
userStories.notes: Spec: All design documents. Performance budget: LCP < 2.5s, TBT < 200ms, CLS < 0.1.
